skill.name: pypict-skill
skill.description: Guidance for creating pairwise test cases that cover combinations of input parameters efficiently.
skill.summary: Generate pairwise test designs for software testing.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported hardcoded URLs and weak cryptographic algorithm patterns in SKILL.md. Review found only documentation text and source links, with no code execution, cryptography, network calls, prompt injection, or data exfiltration behavior.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: The static finding at SKILL.md lines 3 and 20 appears to match the word Pypict or pairwise test text, not a cryptographic primitive. No evidence found of MD5, SHA-1, DES, insecure hashing, encryption code, or security-sensitive cryptographic use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain metadata and a plain description about pairwise test generation. There is no cryptographic API or algorithm reference in the file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Documentation Source URLs
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs at SKILL.md lines 4 and 22 are source repository references. No evidence found of runtime network requests, callbacks, remote loading, credential transmission, or external command execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both URL locations point to GitHub documentation references. The skill contains no executable logic that would fetch or transmit data.

content.value_statement: Manual test matrices can become too large when many parameters have multiple values. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code plan compact pairwise test sets for stronger coverage.
actual_capabilities: Guides creation of pairwise test cases from parameter lists.
Helps reduce large test matrices to focused coverage sets.
Supports test planning for software configuration combinations.
Frames when pairwise testing is appropriate for QA work.
Provides patterns for documenting generated test cases.
limitations: Does not include executable code or a bundled test generator.
Does not validate generated combinations against real application constraints.
Requires users to provide clear parameters and allowed values.
Does not replace risk-based testing for critical workflows.

use_cases.title: Plan Configuration Coverage
use_cases.description: Create a compact set of tests for browsers, operating systems, roles, and feature flags.

use_cases.title: Design API Input Tests
use_cases.description: Map parameter values into representative combinations before implementing automated API tests.

use_cases.title: Review Release Risk
use_cases.description: Compare planned pairwise coverage against known product risks before a release.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Pairwise Matrix
prompt_templates.prompt: Use pypict-skill to create pairwise test cases for these parameters: [list each parameter and its values]. Return a readable table and note any assumptions.

prompt_templates.title: Reduce an Existing Test Matrix
prompt_templates.prompt: Use pypict-skill to reduce this full test matrix into pairwise coverage. Keep high-risk combinations I mark as required.

prompt_templates.title: Add Constraints to Test Design
prompt_templates.prompt: Use pypict-skill to design pairwise tests with these invalid combinations excluded: [constraints]. Explain which coverage tradeoffs remain.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Release Coverage Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use pypict-skill to create a release test plan from these dimensions, risks, and must-test scenarios. Group the output by execution priority.

output_examples.input: Three browsers, two account roles, two payment methods, and two feature flag states.
output_examples.output: A compact test table that pairs each browser with each role, payment method, and flag state across a smaller set of scenarios.

output_examples.input: A checkout workflow with mobile and desktop clients, guest and registered users, and discount options.
output_examples.output: A prioritized list of checkout scenarios with pairwise coverage notes and assumptions about invalid combinations.

output_examples.input: A release checklist that must include two known high-risk browser and role combinations.
output_examples.output: A pairwise coverage plan that preserves the required high-risk cases and fills remaining rows with representative combinations.

best_practices: List every parameter with clear, finite values before asking for test generation.
Identify invalid or impossible combinations before building the matrix.
Add critical business paths manually when pairwise coverage is not enough.
anti_patterns: Do not use pairwise testing as the only coverage for safety-critical workflows.
Do not mix vague parameter values with precise system constraints.
Do not assume generated combinations are valid without domain review.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps plan pairwise test cases for combinations of software inputs, settings, or environments.

faq.question: Does it run a test generation tool?
faq.answer: No. The skill provides guidance and patterns, but it does not include executable generator code.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude Code or Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The marketplace metadata lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What information should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide parameters, possible values, invalid combinations, and any scenarios that must always be included.

faq.question: Will pairwise testing find every defect?
faq.answer: No. Pairwise testing improves combination coverage, but deeper workflows still need targeted and exploratory tests.

faq.question: Is the skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed file contains documentation only and no executable network, cryptographic, or command behavior.
